title: rumex crispus subsp. uliginosus
intro: Rumex crispus subsp. uliginosus, commonly known as curly dock or marsh dock, is a subspecies of the Rumex crispus plant, a perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues and skin conditions due to its high content of anthraquinones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rumex crispus subsp. uliginosus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antibacterial agent, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Rumex crispus subsp. uliginosus include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
